strengths: 19+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, exceeding the 15-year minimum
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes and used by Green Map
Recent AI-adjacent work at re:cinq building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure, plus public speaking and workshop experience (FOSS4G, Selenium courses)
concerns: No recognized AI/ML expertise or demonstrated AI-powered products at scale — AI exposure is limited to ~1 year of MCP/agent tooling
Career has remained at senior/staff IC level with no evidence of principal- or director-level scope, strategic advisory work, or org-wide technical influence
No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, and no evidence of shaping cloud/AI product strategy or advising strategic customers

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise and AI products
Deep, broad engineering experience plus founder/startup background

response.justification: The role requires director-level strategic AI leadership: recognized AI expertise, cloud/AI product vision, and advising strategic customers. The candidate meets the minimum bar — a BS in Computer Science (MIT) and roughly 19 years of engineering experience spanning low-latency DLang microservices at Dunnhumby and full-stack product work — and partially meets the entrepreneurial preference via founding GISCollective. However, the core competency of recognized AI expertise is largely absent: the résumé cites only recent MCP code generators and agentic tooling at re:cinq, with no shipped AI products, publications, or platform-scale AI work, and no advanced AI degree. Evidence of operating at principal/director scope (strategy, executive influence, customer advisory) is also missing; roles are consistently senior/staff IC. Strong senior engineer, but the evidence does not support fit for an AI-strategy principal role.
